title: Genotype-to-Routine Hypothesis Builder
objective: Convert user-provided sequencing notes into low-risk, reversible routine hypotheses that can be tested in a structured personal A/B experiment.
prompt_scaffold: Prioritize routine-level interventions only. Do not recommend diagnosis, prescription changes, supplements with interaction risk, or urgent-care decisions. Translate variant-level inputs into concrete, measurable routine comparisons and explicit confounder tracking.

ui.subtitle: Use sequencing notes to frame testable routine choices.
ui.description: Build a reversible protocol from genotype and home-lab context with explicit safety boundaries.

demo.query: From my at-home sequencing notes, build a personal A/B protocol for sleep routine timing to improve next-day cognitive clarity without changing medications or supplements.
documents: Variant notes from a home pipeline: PER3 rs228697 and CLOCK rs1801260 are present. User reports delayed sleep phase tendency and shiftable wake schedule. Current medications unchanged. Goal: improve morning focus and reaction time.
Wearable baseline summary: 21-day median sleep onset 00:48, wake 08:01, sleep duration 6.7h, morning alertness score median 4/10, resting HR variability lower on days with sleep onset after 01:00.

title: Multi-Omics Crossover Designer
objective: Design an analyzable crossover trial from longitudinal home-lab markers, wearables, and diary signals while preserving low-risk intervention scope.
prompt_scaffold: Treat biomarker and wearable signals as planning context, not diagnosis. Produce a rigorous randomizable crossover design with confounder logging, adherence criteria, and a measurable primary outcome question. Keep interventions routine-based and reversible.

ui.subtitle: Turn longitudinal home-lab data into a structured crossover.
ui.description: Define cadence, confounders, and analysis-ready outcomes for high-discipline self-tracking.

demo.query: Create a crossover design using my monthly inflammation panel trends and wearable recovery scores to compare two low-risk morning routines for sustained daytime energy.
documents: Home-lab longitudinal panel (6 months): hs-CRP ranges 1.2-2.3 mg/L, fasting glucose 89-96 mg/dL, ApoB stable, ferritin stable. No urgent abnormalities reported by clinician. User wants non-pharmacologic routine optimization only.

Wearable exports: readiness score, HRV, sleep efficiency, and resting HR. User notes midday energy dips on low-HRV mornings. Confounders available: caffeine timing, workout intensity, menstrual cycle phase, travel days.

title: Adverse-Signal Clinician Escalation
objective: Handle warning-signal scenarios by producing constrained manual-review or block output with a concise clinician handoff summary and targeted follow-up questions.
prompt_scaffold: If adverse signals or possible safety escalation appear, prefer manual_review_before_protocol or block. Never provide autonomous clinical interpretation or treatment instructions. Create concise clinician-facing handoff language and practical user next steps within platform safety limits.

ui.subtitle: Escalate warning patterns into a clinician-ready handoff.
ui.description: Generate constrained escalation output and focused clarification questions without replacing care.

demo.query: Use my symptom timeline and wearable instability notes to decide whether this remains a low-risk routine experiment or needs clinician review before any protocol.
documents: Timeline: user tracked intermittent palpitations, dizziness episodes after high-intensity exercise weeks, and two nights with poor sleep plus elevated resting heart rate. User asks for a self-experiment plan but wants safety-first guidance.
Wearable flags: resting heart rate increased from baseline 54 to 66 over 9 days; HRV dropped by 35 percent; subjective fatigue increased. No medication changes disclosed.
